title: Ironsight (783770)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Ironsight
- Steam AppID of the game: 783770

## System Information
- GPU: RX 5700 
- Driver/LLVM version: mesa 20.1.7-1/llvm-libs 10.0.1-2 
- Kernel version: 5.8.6-zen 
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Reifam/e79ea050d9dc74c3fd86de3da69341ab
- Proton version: 5.0-9

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## PROTON_LOG dump
[steam-783770.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/5197001/steam-783770.log)



## Symptoms 
Ironsight has apparently been relaunched under a new AppID and this may have different issues than the original release, which had EasyAntiCheat as a main blocker. For me, EasyAntiCheat runs with a loading bar, seemingly finishes that, and then the screen goes white as if the game were about to start in fullscreen. It then just goes back to Steam and the game stops running. It crashes before getting to the title screen. Not sure if this is EAC shutting it down though, because there are ProtonDB reports indicating that players are getting into the game and then encountering other compatibility issues https://www.protondb.com/app/783770

## Reproduction
Install Ironsight's relaunched release and try to start it.

title: Alone in the Dark: The New Nightmare (259190)

title: Teardown (1167630)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Teardown
- Steam AppID of the game: 1167630

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 970
- Driver/LLVM version: nvidia 455.28
- Kernel version: 5.8.16-2-MANJARO
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/JonathanBrouwer/70ad4f8f7aaacf318c4b46caa8f97155):
- Proton version: 5.13-1

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

Proton log:
[steam-1167630.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/5466786/steam-1167630.log)
Std error:
[Std error](https://gist.github.com/JonathanBrouwer/d216cb2fb89a5149a29e902c3a43f386)

## Symptoms 
The game works perfectly (better performance than on windows btw), except when I load a level there is about a 10% chance the game will crash. The log file above contains the crash report.
Reproduces on proton 5.13-1 and proton 5.0-9. Does not reproduce on Windows 10.

## Reproduction
- Start the game, and load into a mission (was not able to reproduce it when quickloading in the lobby world)
- Make a quicksave
- Repeatedly quickload, the crash usually happens within 10-20 quickloads.

title: Wreckfest 2 (1203190)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Wreckfest 2
- 1203190

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6900 XT
- Video driver version: Mesa 25.0~git2503180600.d2e943~oibaf~n
- Kernel version: 6.13.6-061306-generic
- Proton version: Proton Experimental / Hotfix / 9.0-4

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


## Symptoms 

Terrain rendering corruption with black squares as seen in screenshot:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/75316dda-8616-4d29-8ea8-2243d730e62c)

Apparently also happening on Steam Deck as reported by others in general discussions thread.

## Reproduction

- Launch game
- Join any race
- Changing terrain rendering or other ingame settings does not appear to make a difference

[steam-1203190.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19376094/steam-1203190.log)

title: Team Fortress 2 (440)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Team Fortress 2
- Steam AppID of the game: `440`

## System Information
- GPU: RTX 3070 Ti
- Video driver version: 550.40.71
- Kernel version: 6.6.51
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [here](https://gist.github.com/Pao-Lumu/bc83986d44f7ad57151c95ae4afaf8a3)
- Proton version: Experimental, Hotfix, 9.03. etc.

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-440.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17076060/steam-440.log)


## Symptoms 

Game loads to main menu without issue, but upon attempting to load into any map, by any means, the game instantly crashes to desktop.


## Reproduction
Attempt to load into a map (`map itemtest` in console, connecting to community or official servers, starting a listen server, starting training, load a demo, etc.)

Of note:

- The steam_440.log indicates an access violation in client.dll and engine.dll. Likely a difference between Windows and Proton.
- The store and inventory do work fine. There doesn't appear to be any issue with rendering models directly.
- The old demo betas, if launched through Bottles or Protontricks with `hl2.exe -game tf` to work around mismatched executables, can load into maps without crashing.
